Embroidery and lace work I can do, and sew and spin, What is there I cant do, all arts I am proficient in. 2. Clever; expert. Ḍhāk tale kī phūaṛ, mahue tale kī sughaṛ . Prov. The fool beneath the barren ḍhāk tree stands, the sharp one fruit of mahva tree commands. 3. Beautiful; elegant. 4. Good; virtuous; meritorious. Jab bigṛeṅ tab sughaṛ nal, kyā bigṛeṅge kūṛh, Maṭṭke kā kyā bīgṛe, jab bigṛe tab dūdh . Dohā. The good may bad become, the fool no worse can be; the tyre's already soured, the milk perchance may be. sughaṛ-bhalāī , n. f. Currying favor; flattery; wheedling. 1. Yeh bhī āīṅ apnī sughaṛ-bhalāī jatātī huī . Wom. 2. Chhaë dām meṅ larāī, paise meṅ sughaṛ-bhalāī . Prov. Twelve kauris' worth a row can make; sweet flattery a pice will take. …

n. m. S. सौभाग्य from सु good भाग्य portion. 1. Auspiciousness; good fortune. 2. Love; attachment; affection. 1. In mīyāṅ bīvī meṅ to gazab suhāg hai! This pair seem wonderfully loving! 2. Suhāg-bhāg arzānī, chūlhe āg, na ghaṛe pānī . Prov. Wedlock's joys how cheap they are, a hearth without a fire, no water in the jar. (Love in a cottage). 3. Coverture; the conjugal state. 1. Rām kare terā pīlī paṭṭiyoṅ rāj suhāg rahe! Hin. Wom. Ram grant your hair be grey, your sway and coverture endure! 2. Kubjā ko raṅg suhāg dīyo hai, hameṅ bairāg batāī, Ham to jale haiṅ birhā agan meṅ, Kubjā kare ṭhakurāī . Holī. For Kubja nuptial joys, for me the pangs of absence keen, In flames of absence I must burn, while Kubja reigns a queen. 4. Certain ornaments, decoration, etc. worn only during the lifetime of the husband. Jāg, sāṅjhī, jāg, tere māthe lagā bhāg, terī paṭṭiyoṅ suhāg! Girls' song. Sāṅjhī . Wake, Sānjhī queen, awake! good fortune on thy forehead shines, thy parted hair so bright sweet wedlock's joys bespeak. (Only married women may apply red paint where the hair is parted). 5. A marriage song so-called. Idhar kā to yeh rang thā aur yeh rāg, Mahal meṅ udhar ghoṛiyān aur suhāg . Mīr Hasan. 6. Happiness; pleasure; enjoyment. Bāhar tīyāg, bhitar suhāg . Prov. Before the world a saint to see, in secret pleasure's extasy. suhāg baṭṭā , n. m. A medicine used in parsotī or the milk fever of lying-in-women. suhāg-bharī , adj. Happy; beloved; favourite (wife). suhāg-piṭārā , n. m. A basket containing cosmetics, paints, perfumes, comb, bangles, and other ornaments and decorations presented by the bridegroom to the bride. suhāg-puṛā , n. m. A paper folded like a sugar-loaf, containing scents, paints, and fruits for the bride. suhāg chhinnā, yā utarnā , v. n. To be deprived of coverture; to become a widow. suhāg-rāt, suhāg kī rāt . E. n. f. S. सौभागिनी 1. A married woman whose husband is alive; a feme covert . 1. Būṛh suhāgan, sīl sapūtī, kokh, māṅg ṭhanḍī rahe! Benediction. To old age may your coverture endure! blessed be with children good! may womb and mid-hair-part content foreshow! 2. Suhāgan kā bachcha pichhvāre khelne gayā hai . Prov. The married woman's child is gone to play in the yard. (Said to console the mother who mourns the death of her child whose loss may be replaced while her husband lives). 3. Jījī! tauṅh suhāgan bhāgan, maiṅ rāṅḍ, bīr matte terī ke rīs ho saë? Rus. My elder sister! you 're a married woman blest, a lonely widow I, could I presume with you to vie? 4. Sāt suhāgan kī hāth lagā lo! Get seven married women to help (in preparing the trousseau )! (This is considered lucky.) 2. A favorite wife (opp. of duhāgan ). /?/ise pīyā chāhe vohī suhāgin, kyā sāṅvrā kyā gorā . Prov. Who hath her husband's love a husband hath indeed, or be she fair or be she black.
s.f. A married woman whose husband is alive; a woman beloved of her husband; a favourite wife (opp. to duhāgan).

The thief sees naught but theft. 3. Dil ko ho qarār to sab sūjheṅ tehvār . Prov. The heart at rest, all life bright drest. 4. United yet not found, it helpless e'er eludes your grasp. 5. Arat kahahi bichār na kāū, Sūjh juārahin apan dāū . Rāmāyan. The afflicted in their griefs all wrapped, The gambler in his throw enwrapped. sūjh'tā, sūstā , n. m. Management; preparation. sūjhtā k. , v. a. To manage; prepare. more: n. m. Grief (P. sog ). Tan, dhan, dhām, dharanī pur-rāju, Pati bihīn sab shok samājū . Rāmāyan.

def: s.m. Good or auspicious time; goodness of seasons; good season, time of plenty, plenty, abundance, cheapness.
more: n. m. S. सुकाल 1. A good season; a time of plenty; plenty. 2. A good or auspicious time. 3. Morning; dawn. sakāl'e, sakār'e , Rus.; Sant. setā ; Bhoj. samkerāhe , adv. S. सकाल In the morning; at dawn. Tanik sakāre āo karo, bhaiyā, das das paisā pāut hau . Farrukh. Come a little early, man! ten pice aday you get.
s. m. Goodness of seasons; plenty, abundance. ¶ sakāl, see سکار sakār.

def: s.m. name of a class of Pitr̤is or demigods (the manes of the Śudras).

adj. Handsome-necked, &c. ( = su-grīv, q.v.). adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Having a beautiful neck, handsome-necked; — a swan; a goose; a hero; — a sort of weapon; — name of a monkey-king (who, with his army of monkeys headed by Hanumān, assisted Rāmćandra in conquering Rāvan and recovering Sītā); name of one of the four horses of Krishṇ; name of Śiva; and of Indra; name of a particular mountain; name of a particular Nāga or serpent of Pātāla.
s.f. A beautiful girl; a good daughter; — name of a princess (daughter of Śaryāti, and wife of the Rishi Ćyavana).
s.m. A kind of gem; a kind of amber?
adj. & s.m. Doing good, beneficent, benevolent; — easy, practicable, feasible; — doing good, well-doing, beneficence, charity, benevolence.
s.m. A hog, pig ( = śūkar and sūʼar)
subs: s.m. Name of a place of Hindū pilgrimage on the Gomtī (about thirty-two miles south of Ayodhyā-nāth; celebrated as the birthplace of Vishṇu under the form of a boar)
s.m. Name of a division of the infernal regions which is filled with boiling oil.

def: s.m. (f. -ī), A hog; a wild boar (see sūʼar); the hog-deer
subs: s.m. Name of a division of the infernal regions (filled with boiling oil).

def: = śukra; and sūk or suk, qq.v. s.m. An earthen cup or bowl.
s.f. A tractable cow; — the month of May.
s.f. A sow ( = śūkarī, sūʼarī); — a sort of moss, Lycopodium imbricatum.
s.f. (see śūkar), A sow.
adj. Very hard; very unfeeling or cruel.
s.m. = su-kriyā, q.v. s.f. Good action, good work, moral or religious observance, meritorious deed, merit.

ety: S. su-karmās, m. pl. of su-karma
def: s.m. Name of a class of deities.
adj. Well-doing, &c. ( = su-kar, q.v.). adj. & s.m. Doing good, beneficent, benevolent; — easy, practicable, feasible; — doing good, well-doing, beneficence, charity, benevolence.
s.m. = sāvakāś, q.v. s.m. Leisure, opportunity; — adj. & adv. Having leisure, with leisure; — at leisure, leisurely.
more: n. f. S. सावकाश 1. Leisure; time. 2. Opportunity.

ety: inf. n. of سکت 'to be or become silent'
def: s.f. Silence; — adj. Silent; silenced.
more: (from سکت Heb. To be silent) 1. s. f. Silence. 2. adj. Silent.

def: s.m. A good or excellent poet.
s.m. = شکديو śuk-dev, q.v.
more: s. m. The son of Vyāsadeva, the author or narrator of the Bhāgavat and other Purāṇas.

def: n. f. S. संकोचन Contraction. See bhachīṛ & bhīch . sukeṛ k. , v. n. See bhīṅch k .

ety: Prk. सुक्खओ or सुक्खअं; S. शुष्क+कः, rt शुष्
def: adj. (f. -ī), Dry, dried, dried up, parched, withered; emaciated, sapless, juiceless; without advantage or profit; — s.m. Dry land, terra firma; a dry year, dry season, drought; dry tobacoo (eaten with betel-leaf); a consumption, atrophy; the croup
more: adj. S. शुष्क Pr. sokham, sukk . 1. Dried-up; arid ( khushk ). 1. Sūkhe dhān pānī paṛā . Prov. The rice dried up, rain fell. (After death the doctor). 2. Le-ke maiṅ oṛhūṅ, bichhāūṅ, yā lapeṭūṅ, kyā karūṅ? Rūkhī, phīkī, aisī sūkhī mehrbānī āp kī! Inshā. Is this a thing to wear, or spread, or wrap one's self about? Your kindness is so flavourless, and stift, and dry throughout. 2. Shrivelled; thin ( patlā , 3). 1. Adnā, koī ālā, koī sūkhā, koī ḍaṅd pel . Nazīr. 2. Sūkhā sā Bāman ho gayā phūl phāl chugaltā! Prov. The Brahman lean a plump Mogal become! (Said of a parvenu ). sūkhā paṛnā , v. n. To occur (drought). sūkhā ṭālnā, yā ṭarkānā , v. n. To send empty away. Ajī! kauṛī na dūṅ aur sūkhā ṭālūṅ! sūkhā javāb , n. m. A dry answer; a flat refusal. sūkhā dhān , n. m. A rice field burnt by the sun's rays. sūkhe dhānoṅ pānī paṛnā , v. n. 1. To fall opportunely (rain). 2. To get new life; to be …

n. m. 1. Drought; a dry year ( khushk-sālī ). 2. Atrophy; marasmus; consumption. 3. Tobacco leaves dried. 4. Bhang ( Sikhs ). sūkhā lagnā , v. n. To be in a decline. Iskī kāyā ne mere jaisā, Rām kare! lag jā sūkhā! W. Rus. Ram grant her body may wither like mine!

1. adj. Dry. 2. s. m. Dry tobacco eaten with betel-leaf. 3. A consumption, atrophy. سوکهادهان sūkhā dhān, Rice burnt up by the sunʼs rays. سوکها لگنا sūkhā lagnā (dakh.) To be famished, to become emaciated.
subs: s.m. A dry answer; a flat refusal
s.m. A rice-field burnt up by the sun's rays
lit. 'Rain to fall on dry rice-fields'; rain to fall opportunely; to get new life, to be revived
(-ko), To be in a decline, to waste away, become emaciated
adv. By land (as opp. to water).

adj. corr. of sūkshma, q.v. adj. (f. -ā), Subtle, minute, atomic, atom-like; little, small; slender, thin, fine, attenuated; delicate, tender; exquisite, refined; nice; sharp, acute; shrill; ingenious; artful, crafty; accurate, correct, exact, precise; — s.m. An atom; fine thread; subtlety; craft, fraud; an epithet of the Supreme Spirit; — the clearing-nut plant

adj. = sukh-may, q.v.s.v. sukh.

ety: sūkh˚ = Prk. सुक्ख(इ) or सुक्खे(इ), fr. S. शुष्क
def: v.n. To dry, dry up; to evaporate; to be parched; to wither, to pine away, languish, fall away, to become emaciated; to shrivel, to dwindle; — to be kept waiting, be kept out in the cold
more: v. n. S. शोषण Pālī, soso . 1. To dry; dry up ( khushk h .). 1. Sūkhe Sāvan, rakhe Bhādoṅ . Prov. A dry July, a wet August. 2. Jitne bāṛī rang raho, machak machak tolo, Bāṛī sūkhī rang ghaṭo pīyā bhaṭak bhaṭak bolo , Rus. Wom. song. While yet the field is green its bloom the lover keen enjoys, The field dried up, its color fled, dry words he now employs. 2. To shrivel; wither up. Gahrī lālī dekh-kar phūl gumān bhaë, Kete bāg jahān meṅ lag lag sūkh gaë! The flower her deep red vaunting sees and swells with lofty pride, How many gardens in this world have withered thee beside! 3. To become emaciated; to be in a decline; to pine away. 1. Āi rain chakvā sūkhā tīr bin, sarvar sūkhā nīr bin, gorī sūkhī sab hī singār rūkhā hai . Song. Jhūlnā . The duck without her love all night doth pine, the tank of water drained is dry, the fair one thin become no ornaments adorn. 2. Barse daryā sūkhe hīyā, ā…
v. n. To dry, dry up, evaporate, fall away, pine away, emaciate, shrivel, dwindle, wither. See سوکنا sūknā. ¶ sokhnā ( शोषण ) v. a. To soak up, dry up, absorb.
ex: جاؤ دھوپ میں کپڑے ڈال آؤ، کپڑا سوکھنے میں بھی تو ٹائم لگے گا
Go and put the clothes out in the sun, it’ll take time for them to dry too.

def: s.m. Speech, language, discourse, word, words; — thing, business, affair (syn. bāt)
more: n. m. Speech ( bachan ). sukhan-parvar , adj. Bigotted; prejudiced. sakhun-parvarī , n. f. An obstinate adherence to one's word or opinion. sukhan-takyā , n. m. An expletive ( takyā-kalām ). sukhan-chīṅ , n. m. A critic; hypercritic. sukhan-dāṅ, sanj, sāz, shinās, fahm, go, yā var . A scholar; orator; poet. sukhan denā , v. a. To promise; vow ( bachan hārnā , 1). sukhan ḍāl de'nā , v. n. To refuse; reject. sukhan ḍālnā , v. a. 1. To question; interrogate. 2. To ask; beg. 1. Sukhan unhoṅ par ḍārye jo haṅs haṅs rākheṅ mān . Prov. Saying. Beg but of them who smile and show you due respect. 2. Jo mushkil āsān karo to mukh se bachan nikālūṅ. Nahīṅ to chupke raheṅ, āp se phir kuchh sukhan na ḍāleṅ . Sarvar Nir. sukhan-ras , adj. Intelligent; penetrating. sukhan-sāzī , n. f. Fabrication; invention. sukhan kā pūrā . Faithful to one's word. sukhan-goī , n. f. The art of poetry. Sukhan-goī mushkil…
